title: Venezuelan Political Crisis Trump declares 'Venezuelan regime' a foreign terrorist organization, orders oil tanker block
raw_response: {"rating": 1.7, "lean": "Lean Right", "explanation": "This is a news report. The author’s framing and source selection lean in a pro-enforcement, hawkish direction by presenting the administration’s actions and rationale without countervailing perspectives, leading to a Lean Right rating. The result is a +1.7 classification on the AllSides scale. Detailed Analysis: (1) Headline framing emphasizes decisive action (“orders oil tanker blockade”) and repeats “Venezuelan regime” in single quotes without signaling controversy or legal debate, priming readers to accept the enforcement posture; (2) One-sided sourcing: the piece relies entirely on the Trump administration’s claims and actions with no reactions from independent experts, opposition figures, Venezuelan officials, international bodies, or civil society; (3) Justifying background: the author includes detailed description of sanction-evasion tactics (an “armada” of tankers, “ghost ships,” shell companies, transponder disabling) that supports the need for aggressive enforcement, without presenting alternative interpretations or skepticism; (4) Embedded media headline choice (“Energy secretary defends US seizure of oil tanker… as ‘enforcing the law’”) reinforces a law-and-order framing aligned with the administration’s stance; (5) Omission of balancing context: there is no discussion of legal basis and process for FTO designation, potential humanitarian or economic impacts of a blockade, international law implications, or likely diplomatic responses, all of which would provide balance in a neutral report; (6) Minimal qualifying language beyond a single “alleged” in the subhead, with no parallel qualifiers applied to other asserted justifications, contributing to an uncritical tone toward the enforcement narrative. No sources with documented extremist affiliations are platformed in this article."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report. The author’s framing and source selection lean in a pro-enforcement, hawkish direction by presenting the administration’s actions and rationale without countervailing perspectives, leading to a Lean Right rating. The result is a +1.7 classification on the AllSides scale. Detailed Analysis: (1) Headline framing emphasizes decisive action (“orders oil tanker blockade”) and repeats “Venezuelan regime” in single quotes without signaling controversy or legal debate, priming readers to accept the enforcement posture; (2) One-sided sourcing: the piece relies entirely on the Trump administration’s claims and actions with no reactions from independent experts, opposition figures, Venezuelan officials, international bodies, or civil society; (3) Justifying background: the author includes detailed description of sanction-evasion tactics (an “armada” of tankers, “ghost ships,” shell companies, transponder disabling) that supports the need for aggressive enforcement, without presenting alternative interpretations or skepticism; (4) Embedded media headline choice (“Energy secretary defends US seizure of oil tanker… as ‘enforcing the law’”) reinforces a law-and-order framing aligned with the administration’s stance; (5) Omission of balancing context: there is no discussion of legal basis and process for FTO designation, potential humanitarian or economic impacts of a blockade, international law implications, or likely diplomatic responses, all of which would provide balance in a neutral report; (6) Minimal qualifying language beyond a single “alleged” in the subhead, with no parallel qualifiers applied to other asserted justifications, contributing to an uncritical tone toward the enforcement narrative. No sources with documented extremist affiliations are platformed in this article.
